request_summary: Meta-refactor of TemperAI toolkit: reorganize assets/skills/ into per-technology subtrees (backend/<tech>/...), move .NET-only architecture+shared+csharp under backend/dotnet/, add orms/ grouping, and generalize temper-backend to detect tech/ORM from an expanded purely-technical backend-config.md (Framework/Language/ORM/versioned Dependencies). Behavior-preserving for current .NET/EF Core path.

context.architecture: prompt/skill-driven agent workflow; installer flattens nested skill dirs to '-'-joined flat names
context.notes: KEY CONSTRAINT: ClaudeSkillConverter flattens backend/dotnet/api -> backend-dotnet-api, so moving folders RENAMES installed skills. Converter auto-rewrites nested-path references; flat-name/bare-name references (skills-catalog.md, agent load tables, review agent, frontend detection) need manual update. Target taxonomy + multi-tech mechanism + expanded backend-config shape approved by user as direction. NOTE: a prior temper-backend direct-action change remains pending output approval and is preserved, untouched.

approved_plan.description: Read-only impact map: every file hardcoding skill paths/flat names (backend/review/frontend/tester/tasks agents, all SKILL.md cross-refs, skills-catalog.md, architect document-templates backend-config template, temper-architect, installer + tests). Produce folder-move list old->new with resulting flat-name renames, classify nested-path (auto-rewritten) vs flat/bare references (manual). Validate multi-tech mechanism + expanded backend-config shape. NO modifications.
approved_plan.output: Impact map delivered. Key findings: (1) No flat-name COLLISIONS; no installer src/ change needed (skill discovery is dynamic). (2) Converter auto-rewrites only CONCRETE nested-path refs (seg/seg/SKILL.md or bare seg/seg). Manual surface = (a) ~30 BARE 'dotnet-csharp' refs across agents + every 'requires:' list; (b) glob 'backend/dotnet/ef-core/*' refs in api + 4 architecture skills (not auto-rewritten); (c) '[chosen]' placeholders in temper-backend/review. (3) Two REAL-install installer tests go RED: InstallerServiceTests.cs:192 (dotnet-csharp folder) and :213-245 (linq->ef-core-queries). Synthetic converter/asset tests only go STALE. (4) backend-config consumers: temper-backend (92-105), setup-tasks, temper-architect + document-templates scope table (line 26) need new Framework/Language/ORM/versioned-Dependencies fields. (5) temper-backend generalization is behavior-preserving IF emitted strings are concrete new-taxonomy nested paths + defaults resolve to dotnet/ef-core.

approved_plan.description: APPROVED (continue-here). Execute the behavior-preserving reorg + backend agent generalization + backend-config template expansion + installer test updates.
approved_plan.output: Done + verified. 15 skill folders git-mv'd to backend/dotnet/{csharp,architecture/*,shared/*,orms/ef-core/*}; old dirs gone. Non-auto refs fixed (bare dotnet-csharp, requires:/produces: lists, ef-core/* globs, [chosen] placeholders). temper-backend generalized: Phase 1 extracts Framework/Language/ORM, derives tech-root (default backend/dotnet/) + orm (default orms/ef-core/), load tables/precedence/checkpoints tech-root-relative; identity softened. backend-config template expanded (Framework/Language/ORM + versioned Dependencies, dropped PRD-justified External Packages); Document Scope row + temper-architect enforcement updated. setup-tasks unchanged (no consumed field renamed). Installer tests updated (InstallerServiceTests dotnet-csharp->backend-dotnet-csharp, ef-core-queries->orms-ef-core-queries; converter/asset fixtures retaxonomized; single-segment test uses setup-tasks). Docs updated (skills-catalog.md, docs/skills.md, docs/agents.md). VERIFIED: dotnet test TemperAI.slnx 117/117 green. Grep: 0 stale paths in assets/ except 1 intentional illustrative mention (jarvis state-schema:152). NOT committed.

next_action: Plan complete (2/2) + user-requested refinement applied: temper-backend NO LONGER defaults to .NET. Framework+Language are mandatory (hard-stop if missing, no inference); tech-root derived only from explicit values; ORM no default (stop only when a data-access task needs it and ORM is unspecified). Verified 117/117 tests green. Changes NOT committed — awaiting user decision on commit/PR. A prior temper-backend direct-action change remains pending and untouched.
